desc: Kuta, Lombok is the laid-back surf-and-scooter base of south Lombok: a low-rise town backed by scrubby hills and ringed by bright bays, beginner-to-improver surf breaks and long, curving roads through the Mandalika coast. It is less polished than Bali's big resort zones and works best if you want beach-hopping freedom, a smaller-town feel and active days rather than nightlife-first tourism.

todo.name: Tanjung Aan
todo.desc: Spend a beach day on the pale sand and clear bays east of town before sunset crowds build.

todo.name: Merese Hill
todo.desc: Walk up late in the day for rolling headlands and one of South Lombok's easiest viewpoints.

todo.name: Surf lesson
todo.desc: Choose a reputable surf school and beach break matched to your level rather than chasing the biggest waves.

todo.name: Mawun or Selong Belanak
todo.desc: Use a scooter or driver for a wider south-coast beach loop.

todo.name: Mandalika coast loop
todo.desc: Give yourself half a day to string together viewpoints, quieter coves and roadside warungs instead of treating the town beach as the whole story.

todo.name: Early-morning surf or fish-market rhythm
todo.desc: Get out early to catch the town before the heat rises and before the beach circuit turns fully visitor-facing.

wifi.note: Fine in many cafes, villas and established stays, but power blips, smaller guesthouses and full beach days mean it is still much less seamless than a mature digital-nomad hub.

prac.fltNote: Kuta's big advantage is that Lombok airport is genuinely close, so you avoid the long final transfer that haunts a lot of island destinations. From the UK you still connect through Asian or Middle Eastern hubs, and some itineraries fall back on Bali plus sea or air connections, but once you land on Lombok the last leg is easy. Peak dry-season surf months and event periods around Mandalika push villa and driver demand up fast.
prac.lang: Indonesian; English is common in established tourism areas
prac.visa: British citizens need a visa to visit Indonesia. GOV.UK says most tourists can use a 30-day visa on arrival for 500,000 rupiah, with the option to extend once for another 30 days, and your passport must be valid for at least 6 months with 2 blank pages. You should also complete the online All Indonesia declaration within 72 hours before arrival.
bestFor: Couples wanting a lower-key Bali alternative
alerts: Scooter crashes are a common visitor risk; use helmets and avoid night riding.
Some beaches have strong currents and reef hazards.
Town growth is rapid, so the destination feels more dusty-and-developing than the prettiest beach photos suggest.
Wet-season downpours can roughen minor roads.

sweet: June to September is usually the strongest Kuta Lombok window for reliable beach days, cleaner surf patterns and easy scooter-driven exploring, with May and October working as softer shoulder options.
